selected_goal: Ignore preliminary mark-scheme notes and specific marking principles pages when detecting legacy mark-scheme question anchors.
why_this_now: A targeted S20 P51 run showed question 6's mark-scheme crop becoming extremely tall because numbered notes and marking-principles material were interpreted as answer anchors before the real table.
repo_evidence: Legacy mark schemes contain pages titled Mark Scheme Notes, Specific Marking Principles, General Marking Principles, Types of Mark, and Abbreviations.
Those pages can contain numbered paragraphs that resemble question numbers.
The real answer table has a Question / Answer / Marks header that can be used to separate preliminary notes from answer rows.
acceptance_criteria: A notes-only page is not treated as legacy question anchors.
A specific marking principles page is not treated as legacy question anchors.
Numbered notes before the same-page answer table header are ignored while real answer rows remain detectable.
test_expectations: Add tests for notes pages, specific marking principles pages, and same-page notes before a real answer table.
Run the new focused tests and then the question-detection suite.
implementation_boundaries: Do not ignore real answer rows after a Question / Answer / Marks header.
Do not special-case only S20 P51 filenames.
Keep the markers generic to legacy mark-scheme front matter.
repo_hygiene_risk: Medium: front-matter detection can suppress anchors if too broad, so tests must cover both notes-only pages and real answer rows after a table header.
rollback_plan: Remove the preliminary-notes detection helpers and the three notes-focused tests.
done_when: Notes-only and specific-marking-principles pages are ignored.
Same-page answer rows after the table header remain detectable.
